ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob arrived at Peniel, and he began to say: 'I and God have wrestled, but my arm is broken.'

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God brings a person into His path. Indeed, God's path is such that He brings them, both men and women, into it.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: In your time, there are so many stories, and they are both yours and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham: I will increase Sarai, and I will make her Sara.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because I have my own way and my heart, which they say they want to interfere with me. Your way of thinking is different from mine.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob arrived at Peniel, and he began to say: 'God has shown me mercy, and my strength is the strong hand.'

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God brings people with His mercy. Indeed, God's mercy, and He brings them—men and women—and He brings them.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: As for you, there are many true stories within you, and there are your stories and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham, 'As for your wife Sarai, I will multiply her, and I will make her name Sarah.'

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob arrived at Peni-el and declared, 'I have met God face to face, and I have seen with my own eyes.'

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God brings a person into His path. Indeed, God's path is such that He brings them, both men and women, into it.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: In your time, there are so many stories, and they are both yours and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham: I will increase Sarai, and I will make her Sara.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because I have my own way and my heart, which they say they want to interfere with me. Your way of thinking is different from mine.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob came to Peniel, and he said: I have wrestled with God, and my strength remains.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God brings people with His mercy. Indeed, God's mercy brings them; He brings men and women.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: In your time, although there are many stories, only those that come to pass are your and their stories.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham, 'You shall name Sarai, and I will multiply her, and I will make her name Sarah.'

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because I have my own way and strength, which they say I shouldn’t follow, but I do. Your way is not for me; I choose to follow my own path.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob arrived at Peniel, and he began to say: 'I and God have wrestled, but my arm is broken.'
